verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, expressed predominantly in testis/sperm. Unlike IZUMO1 (single-pass TM, sperm surface fusion factor), IZUMO4 lacks a TM domain and is reported as secreted/soluble, but it forms disulfide-linked complexes with other IZUMO family members (notably IZUMO1) on the sperm surface, and has been detected on the sperm plasma membrane in association with the IZUMO complex. Display is restricted to the germline (sperm) lineage. This fits tissue_restricted_surface via stable complex partnership with TM IZUMO1 on sperm. Not yes because expression is narrowly germline-restricted and its own anchor is absent; surface presence depends on partner complex in a specialized lineage.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Unclear whether IZUMO4's sperm-surface association via disulfide-linked IZUMO complex is wash-resistant enough to qualify, vs. predominantly soluble/secreted pool.
